metadata_source: Ubuntu 24.04 apt Packages/Sources indexes under /var/lib/apt/lists, plus source inspection of the extracted noble readstat and tellico source packages
selection_policy: Direct Ubuntu noble dependents only. Scanning the configured Ubuntu 24.04 Packages and Sources indexes found exactly two source packages that Build-Depend on libcsv-dev and exactly two runtime binary packages that Depend on libcsv3, so this list is effectively complete rather than a sample.

dependents.name: readstat
dependents.runtime_functionality: Libcsv is used by the readstat command-line tools only for CSV-input conversion: parsing an input .csv together with a metadata.json description and converting that data into ReadStat-supported outputs such as .dta or .sav. Ubuntu's shared libreadstat library package does not itself link against libcsv.
evidence: Ubuntu noble source metadata shows readstat Build-Depends on libcsv-dev, and the readstat binary package Depends on libcsv3.
readstat's configure.ac checks for csv_parse in -lcsv and enables the HAVE_CSVREADER build conditional when libcsv is present.
readstat's Makefile.am links -lcsv only into the readstat executable when HAVE_CSVREADER is enabled; libreadstat.la itself is built without libcsv.
readstat's README documents that CSV input requires libcsv at compile time and supports `readstat input.csv metadata.json output.(dta|sav|csv...)`.
The CSV-input implementation lives in src/bin/read_csv/read_csv.c and is reached from parse_csv_plus_json() in src/bin/readstat.c.

dependents.name: Tellico
dependents.runtime_functionality: Tellico uses libcsv for CSV import parsing and preview in the GUI: tokenizing rows, handling alternate delimiters, mapping CSV columns to collection fields, and importing LibraryThing-style CSV exports. Its CSV export path is separate and does not call libcsv.
evidence: Ubuntu noble source metadata shows tellico Build-Depends on libcsv-dev, and the tellico binary package Depends on libcsv3.
Tellico's top-level CMakeLists.txt calls find_package(Csv 3.0) and describes it as external support for reading CSV files; FindCsv.cmake searches for csv.h and the csv library.
src/translators/csvparser.cpp includes csv.h when HAVE_LIBCSV is defined and uses csv_init, csv_set_delim, csv_parse, csv_fini, and csv_free to parse imported rows.
src/translators/csvimporter.cpp uses CSVParser to preview CSV content, detect delimiters, map columns to fields, and handle a LibraryThing-specific CSV header format.
The tellico package description advertises import and export of CSV files, but only the import parser is wired to libcsv in the inspected source.
